summary: Iter-140 MUA Phase 2 UI gate verification (frontend-focused review request). Backend already fully validated in iter 128/129 (12/12 pytest cases green). This iter focused on the frontend type-mismatch fix in /app/frontend/app/procedures/submit-phase2/[id].tsx (loadingType string→string[]). Code review confirms the fix is correct: L75 declares `useState<string[]>([])`, L122 coerces both legacy string and new string[] shapes from GET /procedures/{id} into an array, and L558-560 gate uses `Array.isArray(loadingType) ? loadingType.includes('Immediate Loading') : loadingType === 'Immediate Loading'` — which will correctly re-evaluate truthy for an array containing 'Immediate Loading'. Similar dual-shape gates also present at submit-payload (L298 / L304). Seeded 3 Phase-1-approved procedures (All-on-4 Immediate, Single Immediate, All-on-4 Delayed) + implant plans via API and opened each Phase 2 form in Playwright. Browser automation could NOT authenticate the SPA session (expo-web AsyncStorage key mismatch — localStorage seeding of 'access_token' did not satisfy the app's getToken() reader), so GET /procedures/{id} and GET /implant-plan both returned 403 → the loader's catch-branch ran, leaving procedureType='' and forcing MUA to hide in ALL three scenarios. Negative UI assertions (TEST2 Single+Imm, TEST3 All-on-4+Delayed) both reported 'hidden=true' but are INCONCLUSIVE because the same 403 path would also hide them. TEST1 (the positive case — MUA MUST render for All-on-4+Immediate) failed at the browser level for the same 403/auth reason, not a real gate-logic bug.
action_items: Main agent should self-verify the MUA UI by logging in as Gaurav.pandey@student.dental.edu / Student@123 (or student Gaurav.pandey) in a real browser, navigating to /procedures/submit-phase2/69f640120ae04a75cf8d0cb6 (All-on-4 Immediate, Phase-1 approved, implant plan with 4 implants already seeded), selecting Prosthetic Component='Immediate Loading Done', and confirming the blue 'Multi-unit Abutment Placed' section with data-testid='mua-placed-yes'/'mua-placed-no' pills appears. Clicking Yes should render 4 rows of Tooth #11/12/21/22 with mua-angulation-{0..3} and mua-cuff-{0..3} inputs plus inline 'Required' / range warnings for out-of-range values.

Also verify on /procedures/submit-phase2/69f640140ae04a75cf8d0cc7 (Single Conventional Implant + Immediate Loading) and /procedures/submit-phase2/69f640160ae04a75cf8d0cd8 (All-on-4 + Delayed Loading) that the MUA section does NOT render after setting Prosthetic Component='Immediate Loading Done' — proves the gate's procedure-type and loading-type arms still exclude correctly.

If any future testing-agent iteration needs Playwright auth on this expo-web app, use the UI login path (fill data-testid='login-email-input' / 'login-password-input' / click 'login-submit-btn' and wait for navigation) — DO NOT try to seed localStorage 'access_token', as the expo AsyncStorage reader uses a different key scheme on web.
critical_code_review_comments: GOOD: Fix at L75/L122/L558-560/L286-302 is minimal, targeted, and handles both string[] and legacy string shape of loading_type. The coercion `Array.isArray(x) ? x : (x ? [x] : [])` is idiomatic and avoids double-wrapping.

GOOD: The 3-condition gate (prostheticComponent === 'Immediate Loading Done' AND procedureType in full-arch set AND loadingType.includes('Immediate Loading')) matches the spec. The MUA state, muaAngulation and muaCuffHeight, resets on every loadImplantPlan so stale data from a previous case doesn't leak.

MINOR (informational): The submit-phase2 loader's catch-branch (L136-140) silently swallows fetch errors and falls back to single-implant defaults without setting procedureType or loadingType — this made the UI test impossible to distinguish a real gate-miss from a 403. Consider surfacing a toast + 'Retry' button so students don't unknowingly fill the form with wrong implant count when the plan/procedure fetch fails.

MINOR: MUA inline error components use testID `mua-angulation-err-{idx}` / `mua-cuff-err-{idx}` but only RENDER when muaAngleError/muaCuffError return a truthy string. Good gating — no empty red nodes. Consider asserting aria-invalid=true on the input as well for a11y.

/app/backend/tests/seed_mua_implant_plans.py (NEW — posts 4/1/4 implant plans to the 3 seeded procedures so submit-phase2 loader renders the correct torque/MUA row count)

seed_data_creation: 3 Phase-1-approved procedures remaining in DB for main-agent self-test: 69f640120ae04a75cf8d0cb6 (All-on-4 + Immediate Loading, 4-implant plan 11/12/21/22), 69f640140ae04a75cf8d0cc7 (Single + Immediate Loading, 1-implant plan 14), 69f640160ae04a75cf8d0cd8 (All-on-4 + Delayed Loading, 4-implant plan 31/32/41/42). All named TEST_MUA_* so easy to clean up via DELETE /api/procedures/{id} when done.
context_for_next_testing_agent: If re-running UI tests, either (a) perform the UI login flow end-to-end (wait 10-15s for expo hydration; data-testid='login-email-input' is reliable once visible) instead of seeding localStorage, OR (b) intercept page.on('request') and inject Authorization header via page.route(...). The three seeded procedure IDs above are ready to use. Do NOT retest backend MUA persistence (iter 128/129 already green). Focus only on UI gate visibility: mua-placed-yes/no for positive case, and absence for the two negative cases.
rca_of_the_issue: The original Phase-2 'MUA section not implemented' bug was a type mismatch: Phase 1 stores `loading_type` as string[] but submit-phase2 declared `loadingType` as string and compared via `===`, always false → MUA section was never rendered for ANY case. E1's fix (L73/L122/L560 + L286-302) switches state to string[] and uses `Array.includes` with a back-compat fallback to `===` for legacy string data — code review confirms it's correct. Browser-level E2E verification was blocked by expo-web AsyncStorage key-scheme mismatch (localStorage.setItem('access_token', ...) is not read by the app's getToken(), so navigating directly to /procedures/submit-phase2/{id} without doing the full UI login triggers 403 on both GET /procedures/{id} and GET /implant-plan, which makes the loader's catch-branch leave procedureType='' and masks the real gate behavior). Recommend main agent run a quick manual smoke test on the seeded IDs to close the loop.
